title: On A Circle
content:
I'm up and down, and round about,
    Yet all the world can't find me out;
    Though hundreds have employ'd their leisure,
    They never yet could find my measure.
    I'm found almost in every garden,
    Nay, in the compass of a farthing.
    There's neither chariot, coach, nor mill,
    Can move an inch except I will.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jonathan Swift, titled "On A Circle", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
